claim_natural: Dark energy constitutes more than 68% of the universe's total energy density according to the Planck 2018 legacy release.

claim_formal.subject: Dark energy fraction of the universe's total energy density
claim_formal.property: Omega_Lambda (dark energy density parameter) as reported by Planck 2018
claim_formal.operator_note: 'More than 68%' is interpreted as Omega_Lambda > 0.68 (strictly greater). The claim references the Planck 2018 legacy release (Planck Collaboration VI, A&A 641, A6, 2020; arXiv:1807.06209). In the base-LCDM model with spatial flatness (Omega_total = 1), Omega_Lambda = 1 - Omega_m. The claim is about the best-fit central value, not the confidence interval.

B2.label: UNLV cosmic parameters reference: Omega_Lambda = 0.6853(74) from Planck 2018

A1.label: Derived Omega_Lambda from Omega_m via flat-LCDM relation

A2.label: Cross-check: derived Omega_Lambda vs directly reported Omega_Lambda

B1.source_name: Planck Collaboration VI (2020), A&A 641, A6 — arXiv:1807.06209 (ar5iv HTML)

B2.source_name: UNLV Cosmic Parameters Reference (sourced from Planck 2018)

cross_checks.description: Omega_Lambda derived from Omega_m (source A) vs directly reported (source B)

adversarial_checks.question: Has the Planck 2018 value for Omega_Lambda been revised or retracted?
adversarial_checks.verification_performed: Searched for 'Planck 2018 dark energy revised retracted correction erratum'. The Planck 2018 paper (arXiv:1807.06209) was published in A&A 641, A6 (2020) as the final legacy release. No errata revising the cosmological parameters have been issued.
adversarial_checks.finding: No revision or retraction found. The 2018 release is the final Planck data release.

adversarial_checks.question: Could alternative cosmological models give Omega_Lambda < 0.68 from the same Planck data?
adversarial_checks.verification_performed: Searched for 'Planck 2018 dark energy fraction alternative model lower than 68 percent'. Extended models (w0waCDM, curved models) can shift Omega_DE slightly, but the claim specifically references the base-LCDM results. In the base-LCDM model, Omega_Lambda is tightly constrained at 0.685 +/- 0.007.
adversarial_checks.finding: In the base-LCDM model (which the Planck 2018 release uses as its primary framework), Omega_Lambda = 0.6853 +/- 0.0074. Even at the lower 1-sigma bound (0.6779), the value remains below 0.68 only marginally. The central value is clearly > 0.68.

adversarial_checks.question: Is the 68% threshold ambiguous — could it refer to a different quantity?
adversarial_checks.verification_performed: Considered whether 'energy density' might refer to Omega_DE in a non-flat model, or to a different definition. In standard cosmology, 'dark energy fraction of total energy density' is Omega_Lambda = rho_Lambda / rho_critical, which equals 1 - Omega_m in a flat universe.
adversarial_checks.finding: The standard interpretation is unambiguous: Omega_Lambda is the dark energy fraction of the critical density, and equals the fraction of total energy density in a flat universe.
